# Transcrição: Consistency_is_greater_than_intensity

- **Fonte:** Consistency_is_greater_than_intensity
- **Duração:** 03:04
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

I used to have a really poisonous mindset. I used to always think to myself, I am always gonna be the way that I am. I will always think the way that I think and I will always achieve the results that I'm achieving right now. And none of this could ever change. And it's an incredibly sad and negative mindset to be stuck in. If you look forward and understand the fact that you can change just a little bit at a time, if I just think to myself, I can just change just a tiny bit. I can change the level of patience I have. Just by a tiny bit, I can change my durability. Just by a tiny bit, I can change my ability to work hard, my work ethic. Just by a little bit. Over time, what happens is each time you change, you just have to change by a little bit. But then once you've changed that little bit, then again, you move into that new person that you are, then when you're in that new person, change just a little bit again. And if you just repeat this process over and over and over, before you know it, you would have achieved great change. You know, the reason why we often don't try to move towards our full potential and try to grow and we become comfortable with mediocrity is simply because we see change and achieving greatness as being something that is done in just one big step that we're gonna be able to do it in one go. But if we truly understand and become aware that that greatness and achieving our full potential, it's actually less about doing it in one big step, but it's actually about doing it in many little steps. And when we finally understand this, that's when we gain the inspiration and we gain the motivation we need to actually go on the journey and become the best that we can be. In 2016, I made a massive goal. I wanted to run straight for an hour. And completely failed to reach that goal. And when I reflect and I think about why I failed that, it's simply because I saw intensity as something that was more important than consistency. But I think time and time and again in my life, it's proven to be true that consistency beats intensity. The impossible always appears to be impossible, but if you just take things one step at a time, you can achieve anything you desire. I want to be very clear with this. Taking massive action and doing something really drastic, that's really important when you're just starting something. When you're starting a new passion, when you're starting a new hobby, when you're starting a new project, it's really important to take really, really big steps because it gets you really motivated. That drastic action gets you really inspired. But then after that, to continue the big steps, it's just not sustainable. After the big step you initially take, then it's all about consistent action. So remember, you can take a massive piece of action right in the beginning to get you motivated,

## [03:01 - 03:04]

but after that, consistency beats intensity.
